item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, striking down Trump's order
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: NASA awards 3 companies lunar lander contracts to help with moon base program
item.description: NASA has awarded three more companies with contracts to deliver payloads to the moon's surface as part of its effort to build a lunar base. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has the details.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds state transgender athlete bans in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state laws from West Virginia and Idaho that restricted participation by transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports.

item.title: Trump makes millions off of Trump meme coins, financial disclosure reveals
item.description: President Trump's annual financial disclosure report showed he made more than a billion dollars in cryptocurrency last year, including hundreds of millions from selling Trump meme coins. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Trump calls Supreme Court's birthright citizenship ruling "too bad," asks Congress to intervene
item.description: The Supreme Court gutted one of President Trump's signature policies, rejecting his effort to end birthright citizenship. Jan Crawford has more details.

item.title: Extreme heat settling over U.S. heading into July 4
item.description: A heat wave is affecting more than 200 million people across large parts of the U.S. this week and is forecast to persist through July 4. Tom Hanson reports and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: LeBron James to leave Lakers and play 24th season elsewhere
item.description: After eight seasons with the Los Angeles Lakers, LeBron James is switching up his jersey again, opting to change teams for his 24th season. Mike Vorkunov, national NBA and basketball business reporter for The Athletic, joins to discuss.

item.title: States can ban transgender participation in girls and women's sports, Supreme Court rules
item.description: The Supreme Court justices weighed in on whether states can ban transgender athletes from competing in female school and college sports. Jan Crawford has more.

item.title: How Supreme Court's lifting of limits on political party spending could impact midterms
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down coordinated spending caps, unwinding campaign finance limits before the midterms. Alex Isenstadt, senior political reporter at Axios, and Mario Parker, managing editor at Bloomberg, join to discuss.

item.title: Details on Rep. Tom Kean's return to Congress after he says depression caused months-long absence
item.description: Republican Rep. Tom Kean of New Jersey returned to Congress on Tuesday after being absent without explanation for nearly four months. Kean took to the House floor and revealed a depression diagnosis. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has the latest.

item.title: Trump announces first-ever Republican midterm convention in Dallas
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced that Republicans will hold their first-ever national convention ahead of the midterm elections in Dallas.

item.title: From 1976: Alexander Hamilton's early days in the Revolutionary War
item.description: CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces a 1976 segment celebrating America's bicentennial featuring actor Edward Albert and founding father Alexander Hamilton.

item.title: New book looks at GOP influence on state governments and the power of state legislatures
item.description: A new book, "The Fourth Branch," by former New York State Sen. Daniel Squadron, takes a close look at the GOP's growing influence over state policy and his argument that Democrats, generally, have overlooked state governments, to their detriment. Squadron joins "The Takeout" to discuss further.

item.title: LeBron James to leave Lakers, play elsewhere in 24th NBA season
item.description: James, 41, played eight seasons with the Lakers and led the franchise to its 17th NBA championship in the 2019-2020 season.

item.title: Aspen Acres Fire destroys 55 homes in one county, more than 100 structures in another
item.description: The Aspen Acres Fire has destroyed 55 homes in Custer County and more than 100 structures in Pueblo County.

item.title: Age-defying pro-athletes amaze fans, inspire generations
item.description: Forty is not what it used to be, at least not if you're a professional athlete. 44-year-old Serena Williams returned to Wimbledon, albeit losing to a 20-year-old. 41-year-old LeBron James just announced he'll be playing another year, though leaving the Lakers. Mark Strassmann has the story.

item.title: Colorado primaries to test Democratic Party amid rise of democratic socialism
item.description: After big wins for Democratic insurgent candidates in New York City primaries this month, questions about the party's future are taking focus in Colorado on Tuesday. Nick Coltrain, a politics reporter for The Denver Post, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Iran's top negotiator says country ready for war as peace talks inch along
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: DOJ investigating Sen. Ruben Gallego's use of campaign funds, sources say
item.description: The probe focuses on Gallego's use of campaign funds for family trips, a source told CBS News. He has denied wrongdoing.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean reveals depression diagnosis as reason for months-long absence from Congress
item.description: New Jersey Republican Rep. Tom Kean was back on Capitol Hill Tuesday, where he announced his four-month, previously unexplained absence from Congress was due to depression. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Democratic Socialists of America co-chair on New York momentum, new test with Colorado House race
item.description: Democratic socialists are facing a test of power out west in the primary race for Colorado's First Congressional District. Ashik Siddique, Democratic Socialists of America co-chair, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: House GOP agenda stalls over holdouts' insistence on SAVE America Act
item.description: The holdouts blocked Speaker Mike Johnson's plan to merge the SAVE America Act with the annual defense policy bill before sending it over to the Senate.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court's birthright citizenship decision
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday affirmed a more than 100-year-old principle that if you're born in the U.S., you're an American citizen. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Jessica Levinson have more.

item.title: Trump calls Supreme Court ruling on transgender athletes a "BIG WIN"
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday praised the Supreme Court's decision allowing states to ban transgender athletes from competing in girls' and women's sports. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: New student loan rules take effect July 1. Here's what to know.
item.description: Loan revamp affects how much students and families can borrow to pay for college, as well as their repayment options.

item.title: Second Dan Sullivan eligible for ballot in Alaska Senate race, judge rules
item.description: A man with the same name and party affiliation as Alaska Republican U.S. Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible to challenge the senator in the August primary, a judge ruled Friday.
